Title: Allantoin accumulation through overexpression of ureide permease1 improves rice growth under limited nitrogen conditions

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Ureide permease (UPS) family transporter; predicted 10 transmembrane helices
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seedlings younger than 7 days; high in roots, stem and flowers compared to leaves; expressed in vascular tissues
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Down-regulated during N starvation and rapidly induced after N resupply; high in leaves, flag leaves and panicles in activation-tagged plants; altered in leaves, stems and roots in GOS2 overexpression/RNAi lines
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Nitrogen storage and transport compound; can be catabolized to release ammonium for amino acid synthesis; supports growth under limited nitrogen
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Panicles/grains, leaves, stems and roots; especially sink tissues such as panicles and grains
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes allantoin partitioning and accumulation in sink tissues, increases root ammonium uptake, elevates glutamine/asparagine in sink tissues, and improves growth under limited nitrogen
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Activation tagging overexpression; constitutive GOS2-driven overexpression; RNAi silencing; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization in rice protoplasts; in situ hybridization; metabolite profiling of allantoin, ammonium and amino acids; low-N growth assays
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Tetramerized CaMV 35S enhancer in activation-tagging line; GOS2 promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; GOS2-RNAi construct for silencing
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrogen starvation and nitrogen repletion with ammonium sulfate; low-nitrogen soil regimes (100%, 50%, 20%, 0% N); field sampling at 14 DAF
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsUPS1 encodes a plasma-membrane ureide permease expressed strongly in vascular tissues and responsive to external nitrogen status. 2. Overexpression of OsUPS1 increased allantoin accumulation, especially in sink tissues such as panicles/grains, and RNAi altered tissue partitioning with root accumulation. 3. OsUPS1 overexpression enhanced ammonium uptake after N repletion, increased glutamine/asparagine pools, and improved vegetative growth and grain filling under limited nitrogen.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study supports OsUPS1 as a transporter/exporter mediating allantoin loading/partitioning rather than a biosynthetic enzyme or TF. Neighboring putative UPS genes Os12g0502800 and Os12g0503300 were not activated by the T-DNA enhancer. Authors propose persistent allantoin export from roots promotes increased purine synthesis and allantoin production.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Under sufficient N, roots were shorter/reduced length relative to WT, consistent with high ammonium supply; no major yield penalty reported, but benefits were most evident under limited N rather than high N.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for engineering nitrogen-use efficiency in rice by overexpression to increase internal allantoin transport/storage and improve growth or grain filling under low-N input conditions; deployment should consider stronger benefits under N-limited environments.
